entities.name: SOIL MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Practices aimed at improving soil health and nutrient availability to increase crop productivity

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output per unit area

entities.name: KARNATAKA
entities.description: A state in India where soil management increased crop productivity

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where various soil management and fallow management practices were implemented

entities.name: BALANCED NUTRITION
entities.description: The application of nutrients in the correct proportions to optimize plant growth

entities.name: NITROGEN UPTAKE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which a plant absorbs nitrogen from the soil

entities.name: UTILIZATION EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which absorbed nutrients are used by the plant

entities.name: USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The overall efficiency of nutrient use for grain yield and harvest index

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain harvested per unit area

entities.name: HARVEST INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of grain yield to total biomass

entities.name: SOIL TEST-BASED FERTILIZATION
entities.description: Applying fertilizers based on the specific nutrient needs identified through soil testing

entities.name: IMPROVED CULTIVARS
entities.description: Plant varieties developed for better performance and resource use efficiency

entities.name: RAINWATER USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which crops use available rainwater for growth

entities.name: LANDFORM MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Modification of the land surface to improve water and nutrient management

entities.name: BROADBED AND FURROW
entities.description: A specific landform management technique used in black soil regions

entities.name: SOYBEAN
entities.description: A legume crop cultivated in black soil regions of Madhya Pradesh

entities.name: MADHYA PRADESH
entities.description: An Indian state where soybean is grown in black soil regions using landform management

entities.name: BLACK SOIL REGIONS
entities.description: Areas characterized by black soil, suitable for soybean cultivation under specific management

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Practices to maintain or improve the nutrient status of the soil

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: An early maturing legume crop grown in rice fallow areas of India

entities.name: RICE FALLOW
entities.description: Land left uncultivated after the rice harvest

entities.name: LAND USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which land is utilized for agricultural production

entities.name: SOIL-NUTRIENT-CROP-WATER MANAGEMENT
entities.description: An integrated approach to managing soil, nutrients, crops, and water

entities.name: ORGANIC C BUILDING
entities.description: The process of increasing organic carbon content in the soil

entities.name: MICROBIAL ACTIVITY
entities.description: The biological activity of microorganisms in the soil

entities.name: RESILIENCE BUILDING
entities.description: Improving the ability of production systems to recover from stress

entities.name: FARM-BASED LIVELIHOODS
entities.description: The means of living derived from farming activities

entities.name: DRYLAND TROPICS
entities.description: Tropical regions characterized by low rainfall and water scarcity

entities.name: SOIL DEGRADATION
entities.description: The decline in soil quality caused by improper use or environmental factors

entities.name: WATER SCARCITY
entities.description: The lack of sufficient available water resources

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns

entities.name: FOOD AND NUTRITIONAL SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: MICRO AND SECONDARY NUTRIENT DEFICIENCIES
entities.description: Lack of essential micronutrients and secondary nutrients in the soil
